segments.text: Speak unto the children of Israel, and take of every one of them a rod according to the house of their fathers, of all their princes according to the house of their fathers twelve rods: write thou every man's name upon his rod.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: thou shalt write Aaron's name upon the rod of Levi: for one rod shall be for the head of the house of their fathers.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: thou shalt lay them up in the tabernacle of the congregation before the testimony, where I will meet with you.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: it shall come to pass, that the man's rod, whom I shall choose, shall blossom: and I will make to cease from me the murmurings of the children of Israel, whereby they murmur against you.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Moses spake unto the children of Israel,
every one of their princes gave him a rod apiece, for each prince one, according to their fathers' houses, even twelve rods:
the rod of Aaron was among their rods.

footnotes: a rod…: Heb. a rod for one prince, a rod for one prince

segments.text: Moses laid up the rods before the LORD in the tabernacle of witness.
on the morrow Moses went into the tabernacle of witness;
the rod of Aaron for the house of Levi was budded, and brought forth buds, and bloomed blossoms, and yielded almonds.
Moses brought out all the rods from before the LORD unto all the children of Israel:
they looked, and took every man his rod.
Bring Aaron's rod again before the testimony, to be kept for a token against the rebels; and thou shalt quite take away their murmurings from me, that they die not.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Moses did so: as the LORD commanded him, so did he.
Behold, we die, we perish, we all perish.
segments.speaker: Children of Israel

segments.text: Whosoever cometh any thing near unto the tabernacle of the LORD shall die: shall we be consumed with dying?
segments.speaker: Children of Israel
